channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Man in US illegally wanted for allegedly groping 7-year-old Texas girl on her way home from school
item.description: A man wanted for touching a 7-year-old girl in Texas was previously investigated for several sexual assaults of young girls, police said.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Attorney General Pam Bondi rails against New York leaders as she announces immigration lawsuit
item.description: President Donald Trump’s newly installed attorney general, Pam Bondi, is going after New York leaders over the state’s immigration policies.
item.dc:creator: Alanna Durkin Richer And Anthony Izaguirre, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Judge restores Trump administration's buyout offer to federal workers
item.description: A federal judge in Massachusetts restored President Donald Trump's deferred resignation program in a decision Wednesday night after the OPM offered buyouts to 2 million workers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FEMA yanks back $80 million that New York City meant to cover hotel costs for migrants
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Federal Emergency Management Agency has clawed back more than $80 million from New York City, officials said Wednesday, escalating a dispute between the Trump administration and the nation’s largest city over money for sheltering migrants.
item.dc:creator: JAKE OFFENHARTZ and MICHAEL CASEY, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump says he expects to meet with Putin in Saudi Arabia
item.description: President Trump said Wednesday he expects to meet in person with Russian President Vladimir Putin multiple times, suggesting they are likely to meet first in Saudi Arabia. “We ultimately expect to meet,” Trump told reporters in the Oval Office.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: White House acknowledges disagreement with Jordan over relocating Palestinians
item.description: The White House on Wednesday acknowledged disagreement between President Trump and King Abdullah II of Jordon over Trump's pitch to relocate Palestinians from the Gaza Strip while the U.S. takes over the territory.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: House Republicans unveil blueprint to extend $4.5 trillion in tax cuts and lift the debt ceiling
item.description: House Republicans have set the stage for advancing many of President Donald Trump’s top domestic priorities.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: McConnell only Republican to vote against Gabbard
item.description: Former Senate Republican Leader Mitch McConnell (Ky.) on Wednesday voted against confirming former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) to serve as the nation’s top intelligence official, sending a strong message to fellow GOP senators, some of whom privately doubted her qualifications to hold the job.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Senate confirms Gabbard as Trump's director of national intelligence after Republicans fall in line
item.description: The Senate has confirmed Tulsi Gabbard as President Donald Trump’s director of national intelligence.
item.dc:creator: David Klepper, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Russia releases imprisoned American Marc Fogel in what US calls a step toward the end of Ukraine war
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Marc Fogel, an American history teacher who was deemed wrongfully detained by Russia, has been released and returned to the U.S. in what the White House described as a diplomatic thaw that could advance negotiations to end the war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Modi and Trump's friendly rapport may be tested as Indian prime minister visits Washington
item.description: NEW DELHI (AP) — Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s longstanding bonhomie with President Donald Trump could be tested as the Indian leader kicks off a visit to Washington on Wednesday, eager to avoid tariffs that have been slapped on others and threats of further taxes and imports.
item.dc:creator: ASHOK SHARMA and JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump official targeting Jan. 6 investigators worked on those cases himself
item.description: The disconnect between Bove's aggressive stance at the time to hold rioters accountable — and his current hostility towards the Jan. 6 probe — has troubled some former colleagues.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Lucas, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE takes aim at agency that had plans of regulating X
item.description: Musk and other Silicon Valley executives have long railed against the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau for its work overseeing the tech industry. Now, Musk hopes to eliminate the agency.
item.dc:creator: Bobby Allyn, MPR News

item.title: Elon Musk's DOGE has access to these federal agencies
item.description: Trump created DOGE, which he said would “slash excess regulations, cut wasteful expenditures” and restructure federal agencies.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Appeals court rejects Trump administration push to reinstate spending freezes on grants and loans
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A federal appeals court on Tuesday rejected a Trump administration push to reinstate a sweeping pause on federal funding, a decision that comes after a judge found the administration had not fully obeyed an earlier order. The Boston-based 1st U.S.
item.dc:creator: LINDSAY WHITEHURST, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Watch: Musk joins Trump in Oval Office to tout DOGE
item.description: Despite Musk’s pledge to be “maximally transparent,” the White House on Tuesday fired the inspector general for the U.S.
item.dc:creator: The Hill, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: After meeting with Trump, Jordan's king says his country opposes displacing Palestinians in Gaza
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump hosted Jordan’s King Abdullah II at the White House on Tuesday and renewed his insistence that Gaza could somehow be emptied of all residents, controlled by the U.S. and redeveloped as a tourist area.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER, CHRIS MEGERIAN and WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Jan. 6 video evidence has 'disappeared' from public access, media coalition says
item.description: A coalition of news organizations said in a court filing that video exhibits from a Jan. 6 riot case had "disappeared" from a government platform that provided access to evidence used in court.
item.dc:creator: Tom Dreisbach, MPR News

item.title: 27 religious groups sue Trump administration over immigration enforcement policy
item.description: 27 religious groups are suing the federal government in response to the Trump administration’s policy giving immigration agents more leeway to make arrests at “sensitive locations” like churches.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Ventre, MPR News

item.title: What’s next for Jake Sullivan, a Minnesotan and Biden’s former National Security Advisor
item.description: As National Security Advisor, Jake Sullivan helped shape Biden’s foreign policy agenda and was involved in the U.S. response to events like the Russian invasion of Ukraine and the Israel-Hamas war.
item.dc:creator: Nina Moini and Alanna Elder, MPR News

item.title: Mayor Adams thanks DOJ for ending ‘cruel’ bribery case
item.description: NEW YORK CITY (PIX11) – Mayor Eric Adams on Tuesday thanked the Department of Justice after an official ordered prosecutors to drop his case, ending what Adams called a “cruel episode.” On Monday, acting Deputy Attorney General Emil Bove directed prosecutors to drop the case against Adams.
item.dc:creator: Emily Rahhal, PIX 11

item.title: Trump’s ban on gender-affirming care for young people puts hospitals in a bind
item.description: Hospitals and clinics that have offered gender-affirming treatments to transgender youth reacted in a variety of ways to an executive order that aims to halt the care.
item.dc:creator: Selena Simmons-Duffin, MPR News

item.title: A timeline of US-China tit-for-tat tariffs since Trump's first term
item.description: China’s tit-for-tat duties on U.S. imports took effect Monday, just hours after U.S. President Donald Trump announced that he wants to slap new duties on all steel and aluminum imports to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SIMINA MISTREANU, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Las Vegas under partial hiring freeze as city readies for Badlands deal
item.description: Las Vegas expects a settlement soon in litigation over the Badlands golf course, and has already begun to freeze hiring to save money, according to city officials.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News
